**News App**

Submission Instructions

1. **For the News App(part 1\) you should create a separate branch, where there should be a list of news by using the API below.**  
2. **For the News App (Part 2), you should create another branch in the same repository.**  
3. **Your work on the second part should be done entirely on this new branch.**  
4. Your second part should build upon the work you did in the first part and continue the same project.  
5. Regularly commit your work to the new branch and push your changes to the remote repository on GitHub.  
6. Your code should be well-organized, and adhere to best practices in programming.  
7. Feel free to modify the design. There are no limitations as long as you cover all the functionalities. Be creative\!   
8. Your submission should be free of any plagiarism or unethical behavior.  
9. In case of any doubt or query, contact the instructor for help.  
10. Please use high standard code architecture and separation of concerns(use presentation architectures like MVI, MVVM, BLoC) for separation you can use **Clean Architecture**  
11. **Create an application with three SDKs\` Flutter(cross-platform), Android(Jetpack Compose), iOS(SwiftUI), and use all the best practices in every SDK.**

**News App(part 1\)**

For the News app(part 1\) create a separate branch in your repository.

News app(part 1\) should include the list of items from the News API. For getting data you should use [**https://newsapi.org/v2/top-headlines**](https://newsapi.org/v2/top-headlines)**.**   
Please note that you need to register API-Key for accessing data from this endpoint.

![][image1]

**News App** (Part 2\)

* **Filters**

![][image1]

Implement filtering functionality with the following categories: 

- *Business*  
- *Entertainment*  
- *General*  
- *Health*


After tapping the “Filters” button, the user should be able to select one of the mentioned categories and after tapping the “Apply” button, a new api request should be made with a new query parameter, and the list should be updated.

https://newsapi.org/v2/top-headlines?country=us&category=business\&apiKey={your\_API\_key}

* **Details Screen**


![][image2]

The user should be able to navigate to the details screen while tapping on the list items. The details screen should include (at least) the following:

- Title  
- Source  
- Author  
- Description  
- Image

**There is no need** to get an article by id, you just need to pass the model **Article** from one fragment to another to show the data. 

* **Search**


Implement search functionality using the *q* query param. 

https://newsapi.org/v2/top-headlines?country=us&q=aa\&category=business\&apiKey={your\_API\_key}

- If the data is already loaded, but there is no internet connection do the search locally (without API request).   
- A “No Results Found” message should be shown if no data is found.   
- Your search should be done after tapping on the search button  
- Add a “remove” button on the search view so that the user has a chance to remove input text all at once

* **Pull to Refresh**

The refresh action should be triggered when the user pulls the screen down, the app should display a loading indicator and afterward update the existing data. 

Observe the following article: [https://medium.com/google-developer-experts/effortlessly-add-pull-to-refresh-to-your-android-app-with-jetpack-compose-4c8b218a9beb](https://medium.com/google-developer-experts/effortlessly-add-pull-to-refresh-to-your-android-app-with-jetpack-compose-4c8b218a9beb)
